headlines.title: Vallejo police arrest woman suspected in pepper spray road rage case
headlines.description: A woman who allegedly pepper-sprayed a motorist in a road rage attack earlier this week in Vallejo was arrested Thursday, police said. The victim told officers that on Monday, while he was driving south on Wilson Avenue near Hichborn Street, a woman in a red Nissan Altima began making obscene gestures at him. She then aggressively sped in front of his vehicle, blocking him from leaving, according to a release from Vallejo police. The victim began recording the encounter on his phone because he felt trapped and was afraid the woman might be armed, authorities said. The video, according to police, shows the suspect getting out of her car and approaching the victim’s driver-side window. The suspect allegedly sprayed the victim with pepper spray while making threats, temporarily blinding him. The victim then drove away to find safety and wash his face, police said. Officers later identified the suspect and tried to contact her at her home but police said she didn’t cooperate. On Thursday, officers saw her driving near the intersection of Mini Drive and Stanford Drive and pulled her over. But when they approached her car, she refused to get out and allegedly drove away, leading to a high-speed pursuit lasting about four miles. The chase ended when she pulled over in the 100 block of Hilborn Avenue, where she resisted leaving the vehicle but was ultimately detained and arrested. Officers allegedly discovered a large concealed knife hidden between the driver’s seat and the center console of the vehicle. After the arrest, police said the suspect admitted to having pepper-sprayed the victim. She was booked into Solano County Jail on suspicion of crimes that include felony evasion, felony battery, and false imprisonment.

headlines.title: Anonymous bidder pays $9 million to have lunch with Warren Buffet and Steph Curry
headlines.description: Someone paid more than $9 million to have lunch with basketball player Stephen Curry and Warren Buffett, and the legendary investor also promised to match the winning bid so both their favorite charities will benefit. The auction on eBay was intended to revive an event that Buffett hosted for more than two decades that raised $53 million for the GLIDE Foundation homeless charity in San Francisco. This year’s auction that wrapped up Thursday night also raised month for Curry’s Eat.Learn.Play. Foundation that he established with his wife, Ayesha. The anonymous winner paid $9,000,100 to win a private lunch with Buffett and the Currys in the 95-year-old investor’s hometown of Omaha, Nebraska, next month. “We’re overwhelmed with gratitude for this opportunity, which reflects a shared belief that when different generations and institutions come together with purpose, we can create deeper and more lasting impact for the people who need it most,” the Currys said in a statement. The Buffett auctions started in 2000 and continued every year until the pandemic prompted a couple years off. Starting in 2008, every winning bid for lunch with the investing giant topped $1 million. He discontinued the event after someone paid $19 million for a lunch in 2022. A follow-up auction in 2024 raised $1.5 million for a lunch with software titan Marc Benioff, but that version of the event didn’t last. Buffett reached out to the Currys earlier this year to ask them to join the lunch auction this year. Curry missed 27 games this year before returning to help the Golden State Warriors down the stretch. Buffett stepped down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway in January after six decades of leading the conglomerate. He remains chairman, but just sat through his first annual shareholder meeting — sitting in the audience instead of leading the event from stage.

headlines.title: Court bans Kars4Kids ads in California
headlines.description: Kars4Kids, known for its catchy jingle played on television and the radio, will no longer broadcast ads in California, a judge ruled. The Orange County judge found the charity violated false advertising laws. The case stems from a lawsuit in 2021. A man sued the charity’s affiliated organization, Oorah Inc, when he discovered the money from the car he donated was not going to underprivileged children in California. Instead, the money landed with an Orthodox Jewish program in New York and New Jersey that sends teenagers on gap year trips to Israel with their families. Kars4Kids ads will be banned in California until there’s an audible disclosure of the charity’s religious affiliation, the region of where the money goes and a more accurate age range of the recipients.

headlines.title: California governor candidates trade blows at a heated debate in San Francisco
headlines.description: Thursday, seven of the top candidates in the race to be California’s next governor faced off in San Francisco in what will likely be the final major debate before the June primary. Featured on the debate stage were two Republicans (former Fox News host Steve Hilton and Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco) and five Democrats (former Congresswoman Katie Porter, former state attorney general Xavier Becerra, former Los Angeles Mayor Antonio Villaraigosa, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan, and billionaire philanthropist Tom Steyer). With the stakes rising and the race still very crowded, tensions rose to a boiling point between candidates throughout the night. This hour-and-a-half-long debate was hosted by CBS California and the San Francisco Examiner at the Julia Morgan Ballroom in San Francisco. Many of the candidates hurled criticisms at one another throughout the night, talking over each other to the point where it was difficult at times for people to get a word in. The candidates covered a wide range of issues over the course of the debate. Former campaign aide bank fraud charges Becerra, who has been rising in the polls in recent weeks, faced many criticisms from fellow candidates on Thursday, including criticisms in relation to the actions of one of his former aides. Thursday, a former campaign adviser to Becerra pleaded guilty to charges including conspiracy to commit bank fraud related to a scheme to steal campaign funds from Becerra back when he was the Health and Human Services Secretary. “As I have said from day 1, I was not involved in the wrongdoing I had nothing to do with that, I did nothing wrong,” Becerra said. “Don’t take my words for it, take the words of the U.S. Attorney who said no candidate running for governor has been implicated in this particular matter,” Becerra said, referring to a statement sent by the U.S. Attorney’s Office to the New York Times. var pymParent = new pym.Parent('gov-cands', 'https://visuals.otsops.com/projects/ca-gov-cands-2026/index.html', {title: '', parenturlparam: '', parenturlvalue: ''}); Climate and the environment During a lightning round, the candidates were asked if they would increase drilling off the coast of California as governor. Bianco responded, “Yes,” and Hilton responded, “Yes, we have to lower gas prices.” All the other candidates said they would not increase drilling off California’s coast. Bianco said he believes in climate change, but pushed back against the idea of human-caused climate change. “We’ve been threatened since I was a little kid of being in an ice age, of California being nonexistent because oceans were going to rise? It’s not going to happen. Am I naïve enough to believe human beings can affect it? No,” Bianco said. Hilton said the state needs “common sense on climate change, not ideology.” Steyer repeatedly focused on his efforts to take on fossil fuel companies. “They think I am coming for their profits, and they’re right, because that’s the only way we’re going to drive down costs and make California affordable for working people again,” he said. Affordability Each of the candidates tried to paint a vision for their plan to make California more affordable Porter outlined a four-point plan that includes lowering the cost of housing and providing free childcare. “I have plans to lower the cost of housing by building faster, if we build the same speed here in California as we build in Colorado, for example, [we could have] 10 or 20 percent cheaper housing,” Porter said. Mahan said he is focused on lowering costs for consumers. “I’ve called for suspending the gas tax and reforming it so that EV owners pay their fair share for maintaining our roads, ” Mahan said, adding, in San Jose we’ve removed one-time fees that make housing more expensive than it should be.” Villaraigosa said his experience leading a major city would help him to be more effective at cutting costs for Californians. California May 6 Decision 2026 May 6 San Francisco Apr 22 “We need a problem solver, somebody who’s done it before. I was mayor of a city that is larger than San Francisco, San Jose, San Diego, and Oakland,” he said. State Superintendent Tony Thurmond, who is also running for governor, wasn’t invited to be part of the debate stage Thursday, but still showed up to watch and to speak with the press. “I’ve got a very specific plan that I know will help people in the Bay Area and in our state, building 2 million housing units and providing a tax credit to people who are struggling,” Thurmond said. Many voters are still unsure about who to vote for with just a few weeks remaining before the June primary.

headlines.title: Preparations underway for BTS concerts at Stanford Stadium
headlines.description: The popular K-pop group BTS is in town. Around 150,000 fans are expected across three nights starting Saturday at Stanford Stadium. Preparations are underway for the concerts and the merch section ahead of Saturday, Sunday and Tuesday’s concerts at Stanford Stadium. The popular K-pop group has a global fanbase, and this tour specifically is generating a lot of excitement. This is the first time the group’s been back to the Bay Area since 2018. In 2022 BTS announced a break to pursue solo projects and fulfill their mandatory service in the Korean military, but they’re back. “It’s so near me, it’s so close to me, I’m just so excited and it’s been so long and I really can’t wait,” said Dayana, a fan. “My husband printed this on his 3D printer, we actually made like 150 pieces, so I’m going to give them away to the fellow ARMYs,” said Liz, a fan. And BTS is growing a multi-generational fan base by not only attracting their ‘day one’ fans, but adding new ones, too. “It’s dancing, the rap, even though I can’t understand all of it, I like the beat. And from what I’ve seen, their dancing, their performance is,” Vanessa, a fan, said. But it’s not just about the concerts – fans want the merch. “I’m scoping out the scene, scoping out the stadium and where the merch is going to be, I’m coming for the early merch tomorrow,” Vanessa said. As the City of Palo Alto gears up to welcome fans, they’re recommending them to take public transportation. VTA is celebrating BTS’s return with branded buses. And the closest Caltrain station is about a 20-minute walk to the stadium. The concert’s area is also expected to bring in an economic boost. Several hotels on El Camino in Palo Alto said they’re either completely booked or almost booked thru the weekend. “Events like this bring a tremendous energy and vibrancy to our community, and really going to provide a real boost for our local economy, whether it’s hotels, restaurants, small businesses, everyone is going to be able to benefit from it,” Palo Alto Vice Mayor Greer Stone said. Merch opens up Friday at 10 a.m., the parking area opens at 9 a.m. The university is working with a number of agencies to ensure fan safety including Palo Alto Fire, its dept of Public Safety and Entertainment Security partners.

headlines.title: Woman allegedly assaulted by 3 people in San Jose
headlines.description: A woman was taken to the hospital after being assaulted by three people in San Jose Thursday afternoon, according to the police department. Police said officers responded to a report of assault with a deadly weapon in the 100 block of Capitol Avenue just after 1 p.m. There, a woman told officers she was assaulted by three people with an unknown object, according to authorities. Officials said the victim was taken to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. Police said officers surrounded the home and are negotiating a peaceful surrender. NBC Bay Area’s SkyRanger was over the scene, and captured heavy police presence in the area, along with armed vehicles. Just before 8 p.m., police said five people left the house and two suspects were taken into custody without incident. They added the investigation is ongoing.

headlines.title: Santa Clara County officials denounce proposed detention center
headlines.description: Community leaders rallied at the seat of Santa Clara County government Thursday with a flurry of signs and one promise: No federal detention center will be built near Gilroy without a fight. It comes after San Jose Spotlight first reported on public records indicating a real estate firm in Beverly Hills — which has been tied to another proposed detention center in Texas — has won a contract to build a detention center and office space at 7240 Holsclaw Road. U.S. Department of Homeland Security representatives previously declined to confirm or deny the plans, but told this news outlet they are broadly planning to expand detention space for immigration enforcement. Immigration advocates and county leaders indicated they were aware of plans for the detention center before this news outlet reported on it. They warned the plans come amid similar alarms in Dublin, where federal officials are preparing to transfer ownership of a former East Bay prison, raising concerns that city might also be the site of a federal detention center. The news conference saw a large crowd of people who waved signs condemning the plans and immigration enforcement authorities in general. The crowd included organizers with the Immigrant Protection & Empowerment Network, Services, Immigrant Rights and Education Network, Amigos de Guadalupe and others. Members of San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan’s office also attended, as well as his wife, Sylvia Scandar Mahan. Mahan was not present. Santa Clara County leaders said they’ve been contacted by California Attorney General Rob Bonta’s office. “Although he couldn’t be here today, his office has reached out and expressed their support and are actively coordinating with us on this case,” County Counsel Tony LoPresti said at the news conference. LoPresti vowed to fight the detention center with the full resources of his office, as the county property is not zoned for a detention center — but warned the federal government could try to establish precedence of federal laws over local ones. Still, he pointed to the county’s past lawsuits against President Donald Trump’s administration. The county led a coalition of local jurisdictions, including San Francisco, suing to stop attempts to cut funding to cities and counties who declare themselves sanctuaries for people without citizenship. The county has also taken steps to coordinate real-time responses to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement operations and ban immigration authorities from using county property for enforcement activity. “We have been successful and effective at virtually every single turn,” LoPresti said. “Yes, we talk a big talk — but we have walked the walk. And you better believe we’re going to keep walking.” District 1 Supervisor Sylvia Arenas, who represents South County where the detention center is planned, said she expects the Trump administration to undermine the legal process. But she’s undeterred. “We shall see what ends up happening, but we won’t regret any effort we’ve taken to fight this,” Arenas told San Jose Spotlight. A confluence of Latino community organizers, South County business owners and San Jose officials emphasized that while records point to a detention center, it’s unclear what exactly will be built along Holsclaw Road. “While we do not yet have definitive information about the future of this site, we know enough to understand what is at stake,” San Jose Councilmember Peter Ortiz said at the news conference. “We have already seen the harm that aggressive immigration enforcement and detention policies have caused. We have seen families destabilized. We have seen communities pushed into fear and isolation. We have seen parents afraid to take their children to school.” Maritza Maldonado, founder and executive director of Amigos de Guadalupe, vowed to block ICE’s potential expansion. “We, together, are ready,” Maldonado said at the news conference. “We will organize, we will protect, we will advocate and we will litigate.” Editor’s note: This story originally appeared on San Jose Spotlight.

headlines.title: Man kills his brother and sets house on fire in Santa Clara, police say
headlines.description: Santa Clara police revealed new information on Thursday regarding the deadly shooting and fire last Friday in the usually peaceful Enright Avenue neighborhood. Santa Clara police responded to a call of multiple gunshots on Enright Avenue on May 8. When they arrived, they found not only one person shot, but also a home on fire. Thursday, police revealed the victim was 52-year-old Mark Heflin and the shooter was his estranged brother Matthew Heflin, who investigators believe traveled from Bloomington, Indiana and spent several weeks in the San Jose area before the crime. Heflin rented a home right next to his brother without telling anyone in the family. Santa Clara May 9 Santa Clara May 8 That’s where police believe he waited until May 8, when he spotted his brother and confronted him. “After a brief verbal exchange, the suspect, who was concealing a firearm inside of a paper bag, opened fire. Crime scene investigators recovered 12 spent shell casings from the area in front of the residence. The suspect then retreated through a side yard of the rental property, fired additional rounds towards the victim’s vehicle and home, returned inside the rental and intentionally set the house on fire in a rear bedroom,” said Santa Clara police Chief Cory Morgan. Police said Heflin then started a fire in the home and took his own life. “Inside the burned residence, investigators recovered the suspect’s passport, ammunition and manufactured fire logs, approximately $95,000 in U.S. currency and four pistols,” Morgan said. Mayor Lisa Gillmor conveyed condolences to the victim’s family. Police are still investigating a big question that remains: they know what the brother did they still don’t know why.

headlines.title: 49ers' 2026 NFL schedule release reveals game dates, kickoff times, TV info
headlines.description: It’s here, 49ers Faithful. San Francisco’s full schedule for the 2026 NFL season officially has been released. It already was known that the 49ers would open their season against the Los Angeles Rams in Melbourne, Australia. While they will be the “home” team for that game down under, they will actually host their next three games at their real home, Levi’s Stadium. San Francisco will face the Miami Dolphins, Arizona Cardinals and Denver Broncos in Weeks 2, 3 and 4, respectively, in Santa Clara. The 49ers’ official first road game will come against no other than the reigning Super Bowl champion Seattle Seahawks in Week 5 on Oct. 11. They will have a Week 8 bye. San Francisco will host the 2026 No. 1 pick, Fernando Mendoza, and the Las Vegas Raiders the following week on Nov. 8. at Levi’s Stadium. The 49ers, who will be the most traveled NFL team in 2026, will face the Minnesota Vikings in their Mexico City game, their second international game of the season, in Week 11 on NBC’s “Sunday Night Football” at 5 p.m. PT at Estadio Banorte. San Francisco will close out the regular season with matchups against the Kansas City Chiefs, Philadelphia Eagles and Arizona Cardinals. In total, the 49ers will have five prime-time matchups: Thursday Night Football vs. Rams (Week 1), Monday Night Football vs. Commanders (Week 6), Sunday Night Football vs. Vikings (Week 11), Thursday Night Football vs. Chargers (Week 15) and Sunday Night Football vs. Eagles (Week 17). Download and follow the 49ers Talk Podcast This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: PG&E officials questioned over apparent lapses before massive SF blackout
headlines.description: At a Board of Supervisors Committee hearing Thursday, PG&E officials were questioned over apparent lapses before a massive fire and blackout at a San Francisco substation last year. That incident left 130,000 people in the dark on Dec. 20. Last week, an independent review suggested it was sparked by condensation and dust build-up on equipment inside the mission substation, leading to short circuiting. Senior Investigator Jaxon Van Derbeken was at the meeting. He has the full report in the video player above.

headlines.title: Boys and Girls Club to open first playground on Oakland's International Boulevard
headlines.description: One of Oakland’s roughest streets is getting a much-needed addition that aims to give the city’s youth a safe place to simply be kids. It’s the first playground on Oakland’s International Boulevard, and the first outdoor play structure of any of the Oakland Boys and Girls Club branches. An outdoor safe space in a challenging corridor that’s made especially just for kids. “It’s a piece of their childhood that they don’t get to have and most of our members have to travel in order to get to a park. That is a day experience, rather than just a part of their day,” said BGCO Carr Branch Director Leiani Celestan. After months of planning, the playground was built in a day transforming a blacktop into a colorful space and mural celebrating Oakland’s history. The project was a partnership between the Boys and Girls Club of Oakland, Kaboom!, and Sidley Law Firm, which funded the playground. 50 lawyers joined community volunteers on Wednesday to help build the playground by hand. “It seems like something so basic, but getting a playground, a safe space — an inspiring space for our kids to play in — is really important and every child deserves that,” said Sidley Law Firm Managing Partner (San Francisco Sharon Flanagan. Organizers hope the visible playground helps reshape the image of International Boulevard that has been long known for crime and less than kid-friendly activity — while giving families a place that feels welcoming and safe. “In this community being able to see playgrounds, safe spaces for your kid to play is not something that is common. It provides a curiosity point for our community, a safe space for people to come in, see what this is about and also allow their kid to play here,” Celestan said. Kids can’t play on the playground just yet; it will take a couple more days. But on Monday, this will be filled with students enjoying the great outdoors.

headlines.title: SF supervisor proposes easing shadow review rules to speed housing
headlines.description: A San Francisco supervisor says he wants to “shine a light on the shadows” in hopes of creating more places to live in the city. Supervisor Bilal Mahmood says data shows just how much housing has been delayed or stalled by “shade” over the past 10 years, and believes if his newly proposed legislation had been in effect, many of those units could have been built faster. Mahmood rolled out a new proposal Thursday that could quicken housing approvals, in part, by eliminating so-called shadow analysis rules. Those rules, included in the California Environmental Quality Act, allow people to appeal new construction. The supervisor says the name of his legislation spells it out; the Slashing Housing Appeals of Delays Everywhere, or SHADE Act. “The SHADE Act is addressing this unique to San Francisco issue where we require shadow to be used in our environmental review process here in San Francisco. We’re the only major city in the entire state that has this strict of a requirement,” Mahmood said. The supervisor plans to formally introduce the legislation next week, and says it would force a new look at environmental reviews that exceeds the state mandate and has slowed housing projects.
